preamble: The elision of the back unrounded vowel [ʌ] typically occurs through a process of reduction. When a syllable loses stress, [ʌ] centralizes to a schwa [ə] and is eventually deleted entirely (syncope).

examples.note: In extremely rapid speech, the STRUT vowel can reduce to zero, particularly before sonorants.
In rapid or dialectal Russian, pre-tonic vowels reduced to [ʌ] via akan'e can be further elided entirely.

sources: Ladefoged, P. & Johnson, K. (2015). A Course in Phonetics (7th ed.). Cengage Learning.
